title: 1. Infrastructure observability
description: Errors summary at top (API error rate, exceptions, recent logs), then CPU, throughput, OOM, pod health, auto-scaling, and API error rates.

options.content: 1. Infrastructure2. Network3. AI4. Data5. User6. Cost & Usage7. Safety & Security

panels.title: Errors (5m)
panels.description: Failures in the last 5 minutes. Shown beside the tabs.

panels.title: Errors summary — API error rate
panels.description: Failed requests in the last 5 minutes. Green is healthy.

panels.title: Errors summary — Exceptions (1h)
panels.description: Total failures in the last hour. Zero or low is normal.

panels.title: Errors summary — By type
panels.description: Which error happened most. Longest bar = main problem.

panels.title: Errors summary — By category
panels.description: What kind of problem it is. Biggest slice = check that first.

panels.title: Errors summary — Recent error logs

targets.expr: {service_name=~".+"} | json | event_type="telemetry_event" | department=~"$department" | model_name=~"$model" | status="error" | line_format "{{.timestamp}} [{{.model_name}}] {{.operation_name}} type={{.error_type}} cat={{.error_category}} status={{.http_status_code}} dept={{.department}} lat={{.latency_ms}}ms"

panels.description: Latest failed requests. Newest at the top.

panels.title: CPU utilization
panels.description: How hard the gateway servers are working. High means they are under heavy load.

panels.title: Model throughput
panels.description: How many AI output tokens the system produces per second.

panels.title: OOM failures
panels.description: Times a container ran out of memory and was killed in the last 24 hours.

panels.title: Pod/container health
panels.description: How many gateway pods are up and ready. 100% means all are healthy.

panels.title: Auto-scaling events
panels.description: How often the system added or removed pods in the last 24 hours.

panels.title: API error rate
panels.description: Percent of requests that failed. Lower is better.

CPU, throughput, pod health, and error trends

panels.title: HPA Scaling
HPA Current vs Desired Replicas
panels.description: How many pods are running vs how many the system wants.

panels.title: Pod Restart Rate
panels.description: How often pods restart. Frequent restarts may mean crashes.
Replica counts and pod restart activity

panels.title: Container Resources
Container Memory RSS by Pod
panels.description: Memory used by each gateway pod.

panels.title: Container CPU Usage by Pod
panels.description: CPU used by each gateway pod.

panels.title: Node Memory Available Over Time
panels.description: Free memory on cluster machines over time.

panels.title: Node CPU Usage (user mode)
panels.description: CPU used by applications on each cluster machine.
Per-pod and node-level resource usage

panels.title: Runner Self-Observability
Batch Duration Heatmap
panels.description: How long each telemetry batch took to process.

panels.title: Kafka Queue Depth
panels.description: How many events are waiting to be sent. A growing queue means backlog.

panels.title: Runner Event Publish Rate
panels.description: How many events the system sends per second.

panels.title: Batch Duration p99
panels.description: Slowest 1% of batch processing times. High means occasional delays.
Telemetry runner batching and Kafka queue depth

panels.title: OTel Collector
Collector Exporter Queue Size
panels.description: How full the telemetry export queues are.

panels.title: Collector Export Throughput & Failures
panels.description: How much telemetry is exported and how many exports fail.
OpenTelemetry collector export queues and failures
